steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: the first defines Nobel Prize criteria, the second gives a 2004 example. A semicolon is the correct connector, and the closing quotation mark precedes it per American English convention.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — direction": leaves two independent clauses joined with nothing, creating a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — direction" and,: uses punctuation that does not correctly separate the two independent clauses in this context. ✗
  • Choice C — direction,": creates a comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — direction";: correctly places the closing quotation mark before the semicolon and uses the semicolon to join the two independent clauses: "direction"; in 2004, for instance... ✓
That is the correct answer.
